system.description: True for built-in system route types.

carrierStatus.description: Status returned by the LIDB network. 'Success' indicates the request was accepted; other values describe the failure. Local cache is updated regardless of this field.

mms.description: Admin only. Whether MMS messaging is enabled.

sms.description: Admin only. Whether SMS messaging is enabled.

ticketCreateRequest.description: Request body for the ticket Create operation.

subject.example: I need help with my account

email.description: Admin only: create ticket on behalf of this customer email.

portFeature.description: Per-TN feature configuration applied after the port completes.

gatewayId.description: Gateway ID to route this TN after porting.

portSubmitRequest.description: Request body for the port Submit operation.

did.description: One or more 10-digit phone numbers to port. Toll-free (8xx) numbers are separated from geographic numbers automatically.

name.description: Subscriber name exactly as it appears on the bill from the losing carrier.

lcBtn.description: Billing telephone number from the losing carrier bill.

lcAccountNumber.description: Account number from the losing carrier bill.

pin.description: Port-out PIN. Required by many carriers especially mobile. Contact the losing carrier to obtain.

streetNumber.description: Street number as it appears on the service address.

streetType.description: USPS street type abbreviation (e.g. ST, AVE, BLVD).

unitValue.description: Optional unit designator (e.g. APT, STE) and number.

authPerson.description: Full name of the person authorized to sign the LOA.

desiredDueDate.description: Optional desired port date (YYYY-MM-DD). Leave blank for standard processing time.

features.description: Optional per-TN routing, LIDB, and SMS configuration applied after the port completes.

portSubmitResponse.description: Response wrapper for the port Submit operation.

loaUrl.description: URL to download and sign the Letter of Authorization.

portUrl.description: URL to track port order status.

message.example: Port request a3a2a submitted. Please complete the LOA to proceed.

inventoryItem.description: A single telephone number available for assignment.

lata.description: Local Access and Transport Area code.

numbers.description: Available numbers matching the query (may be empty).

inventoryCoverageItem.description: A single coverage bucket. Which fields are populated depends on the countBy dimension.

count.description: Number of available TNs in this bucket.

coverage.description: Aggregated availability buckets matching the chosen dimension and filters.

portSummary.description: Summary row from the customer port-status list.

id.description: Stable port record identifier used to fetch full detail.

pid.description: Short human-readable port reference shown to support.

message.description: Free-form message from the port team (schema portSummary).
message.example: Your numbers will be porting on May/04/2026 at 10:00 AM Central.

supportUrl.description: URL to the related support ticket conversation.

createdAt.description: Unix timestamp the port was submitted (schema portSummary).

portListResponse.description: Wrapper around the user's port-in records.

ports.description: Port records owned by the authenticated account, newest first.

portDetail.description: Full detail for a single port-in record.

message.description: Free-form message from the port team (schema portDetail).

numbers.description: Telephone numbers in this port order.

name.description: Name on the porting account at submission.

createdAt.description: Unix timestamp the port was submitted (schema portDetail).

portDetailResponse.description: Wrapper around a single port-detail document.

portOutPinUpdateRequest.description: Set the port-out PIN that future losing carriers must present to release this number.

portOutPinUpdateResponse.description: Confirmation that the port-out PIN was updated on the network.

number.description: Ten-digit telephone number whose PIN was set.

e911CreateRequest.description: Address details to validate and provision for an e911 record.

dn.description: Ten-digit telephone number owned by the authenticated account.

messageSendRequest.description: Body to send an SMS or MMS. type is inferred from mediaUrls.

from.description: Source 10-digit number; must be on the authenticated account.

text.description: Message body (UTF-8). Long messages may be split into multiple segments.

mediaUrls.description: One or more publicly-reachable media URLs. Presence makes this an MMS.

messageSendResponse.description: Confirmation that the message was accepted by the messaging network.

id.description: Provider transaction id; use as a search key in delivery-receipt webhooks.

mediaUrls.description: Echo of submitted media URLs (MMS only).

messageHistoryResponse.description: Message Detail Records for a number within a time range.

to.description: Unix timestamp range end (older than from).

messages.description: MDR rows for this number/range, newest first.

numberMessagingState.description: Current messaging routing state for a single number, including the bound campaign and its CSP status if any.

enabled.description: Whether the number can currently send or receive messages.

network.description: Messaging path the number is currently on. null when carrier is 0 or any non-messaging carrier.

carrier.description: Internal carrier identifier on the numbers row.

routeIn.description: numbers_sms row id used for inbound routing; 0 means no route configured.

resource.description: URL or endpoint that receives inbound messages for this routeIn.

onAccount.description: Present and false when the number is not on the authenticated account.

campaign.description: Campaign currently bound to this number, with its CSP-side status. null when no binding exists.

status.description: CSP campaign status: ACTIVE, EXPIRED, SUSPENDED, …

upstreamCnpId.description: SFL9UTQ = path A, SB8TWLO = path B.

numberMessagingPatchRequest.description: Update inbound or outbound routing on a single number. At least one of routeIn / routeOut must be set.

routeIn.description: numbers_sms row id for inbound. 0 to detach.

orderCreateRequest.description: Body for POST /v2.2/orders. numbers may be plain strings or objects with optional route.

numbers.description: One or more numbers (max 100). Each may be a string or an object {number, route?}.

route.description: Optional gateway_id on this account; defaults to 4.

orderCreateResponse.description: Result of an order. amountCharged is what was debited from the account.

numbersOrdered.description: Numbers inserted into the account on success.

portAvailabilityResponse.description: Portability check result for one number.

portable.description: True if the network reports this number can be ported in.

losingCarrier.description: SPID/OCN of the current carrier when reported.

messagingBrandCreateRequest.description: Body passed through to the brand registration service.

messagingCampaignCreateRequest.description: Body passed through to the campaign registration service. campaignClassName and campaignStartDate are auto-populated if omitted.

numberCampaignAssignRequest.description: Assign a 10DLC campaign to this number.

upstreamCnpId.description: SFL9UTQ = path A, SB8TWLO = path B (from CSP sharing).

unassigned.description: Numbers whose binding was successfully removed.

bearerAuth.description: 32-character hexadecimal API key obtained by POSTing your username and password to /v2.2/account/apikey. Pass it as the Bearer token on every request. Re-fetch after any password change.

post.description: Creates a new account. No authentication required. Returns account credentials and API key. A verification email is sent.

400.description: Bad request response from account create.

requestBody.description: Body for create a new account.

get.description: Returns full account details including balance, services, and rates. Rates are read-only. Rate limited: 6 requests per hour per IP.

put.description: Updates account settings. Customers may update: timezone, notify, notifyThreshold, callerId. Administrators may additionally update: ccs, service flags, and rate fields.

400.description: Bad request response from account put.

post.description: Creates a new sub-account. Requires administrator credentials. The new account password is auto-generated and returned.

Returns the current API key for the account identified by username and password. No Bearer token is required or accepted — credentials must be provided in the request body. Rate limited to 6 requests per hour per IP.

get.description: Call Detail Records for the authenticated account within the given Unix-timestamp range. Rate-limited to 6 requests per hour per IP.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Range start as Unix timestamp. Defaults to now.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Range end as Unix timestamp. Defaults to 24h ago.

get.description: Returns the complete credit history for the account — all credits regardless of invoice status. Each entry includes a paid flag: true means the customer has fulfilled the invoice; false means the invoice is still outstanding. Use /v2.2/account/credits/paid or /v2.2/account/credits/unpaid to retrieve a filtered subset.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter results to credit entries with the given paid/unpaid state.

get.description: Returns current MRC broken down by service plus a total. Rate limited: 6 requests per hour per IP.

200.description: OK response from account recurring charges.

get.description: Returns all PayPal payments received on the account, newest first. Each entry includes the amount, date, PayPal transaction ID, payment status, and the payer email address. Payments represent real cash received; for credit adjustments see GET /v2.2/account/credits.

post.description: Sends a password recovery link to the email address on file. No authentication required.

404.description: Email not found

get.description: Returns the current SIP URI and user agent for the registered device.

404.description: Not registered

get.description: Returns all IP CIDR blocks on the account IP authentication ACL.

404.description: No entries found

delete.description: Removes one or more IPv4 CIDR blocks from the IP-ACL. CIDRs are passed as a comma-separated `cidrs` query string parameter.

409.description: One or more entries could not be removed

error.message: One or more CIDRs not on the ACL

parameters.name: cidrs
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of CIDR blocks to remove from the ACL (e.g. 203.0.113.0/24,203.0.113.1/32).

post.description: Adds one or more IPv4 CIDR blocks. Only routable public addresses accepted. Mask must be /8, /16, /24, or /32.

409.description: One or more entries could not be added

error.message: CIDR overlaps with existing ACL entry

requestBody.description: Body for add ip acl entries.

get.description: Returns the current authentication type, its description, and all IP ACL entries for the account.

put.description: Updates the account authentication type and/or password.

400.description: Bad request response from auth put.

get.description: Returns all e911 records provisioned for telephone numbers on the account.

200.description: OK response from e 911 query all.

404.description: No records found

post.description: Validates the address and provisions an e911 record for the given dn. dn must be a 10-digit telephone number owned by the authenticated account.

requestBody.description: e911 record details. The dn field identifies which number this record applies to.

400.description: Required e911 field missing or malformed

403.description: Number is not on the authenticated account (POST /v2.2/e911).

409.description: e911 service rejected the address or provisioning

post.description: Validates a street address for e911 provisioning. Returns the validated address including an addressid for use with PUT /e911/{dn}.

put.description: Provisions an e911 address using a pre-validated addressId from POST /e911/validate.

parameters.name: dn
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number for the e911 record (PUT /v2.2/e911/{dn}).

requestBody.description: Body for provision e911 with known addressid.

parameters.description: 10-digit telephone number — used by e 911 query record.
parameters.name: dn
dn
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number for the e911 record (DELETE /v2.2/e911/{dn}).

204.description: No Content response from e 911 remove record.

parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by gateways put.

parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by gateways numbers.

put.description: Sets the outbound Caller ID Name (CNAM/LIDB) for the number. Writes the name to the local cache (used by the softswitch for outbound caller-id) and then submits the update to the LIDB network. The local cache update is durable; a downstream failure is reported via the response's carrierStatus field but does not fail the request. Max 15 alphanumeric characters.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number — used by numbers libd.

requestBody.description: Body for set outbound caller id name (lidb).

post.summary: Release a number back to the network
post.description: Releases the number back to the network (different from removing it from the account). A release event is created; the number cannot be re-assigned.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number — used by number release.

parameters.name: number

get.description: Returns support tickets. Customers see only their own tickets. Administrators see all tickets.

post.description: Creates a new support ticket. The customer email is automatically populated from the authenticated account. Administrators may optionally specify a different email.

requestBody.description: Body for create a support ticket.

put.description: Updates the status of a ticket. Customers can only update their own tickets.

parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by tickets put.

parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by tickets get.

404.description: Not Found response from tickets get.

parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by tickets delete.

parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by support ticket reply create.

403.description: Forbidden response from support ticket reply create.

200.description: OK response from support ticket messages.

403.description: Forbidden response from support ticket messages.

get.description: Returns up to `quantity` numbers matching the tnMask and optional filters. Results are cached for 10 minutes.

parameters.name: tnMask
parameters.description: Prefix or wildcard pattern; `201`, `201555`, `201555*`.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: Maximum results, 1–1000. Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: province
parameters.description: Limit results to this 2-letter US state or Canadian province code.

parameters.name: rateCenter
parameters.description: Rate-center name.

parameters.name: lata
parameters.description: LATA code.

502.description: Network did not return a usable response

get.description: Returns counts of available numbers bucketed by the chosen dimension. Optional filter parameters narrow the result. Results are cached for 10 minutes.

parameters.description: Aggregation dimension.

parameters.name: locState
parameters.description: Filter: state or province code (e.g. NJ).

parameters.name: city
parameters.description: Filter: city name.

parameters.description: Filter: rate-center abbreviation.

parameters.description: Filter: area code.

get.description: Returns every port-in record submitted by the authenticated account, newest first.

200.description: Port records owned by this account

ports.message: Your numbers will be porting on May/04/2026 at 10:00 AM Central.

401.description: Authentication failed for the port list

post.description: Submits a number port-in. Replicates the legacy LNP web form: creates a port record, opens a support ticket, notifies the LNP team, and deducts $6 per TN from accounts.cash. Numbers must have been confirmed portable via GET /v2.2/ports/availability/{number} within the prior hour. The LOA must be uploaded after submission.

requestBody.description: Address, account, and TN list for the port-in submission.

201.description: Port record created and support ticket opened

400.description: Validation failed before any side effect

get.description: Returns the full detail for one port-in record. Returns 404 if the id does not exist or is owned by a different account.

port.name: Michael Mavroudis
port.message: Your numbers will be porting on May/04/2026 at 10:00 AM Central.

404.description: No port record matches that id under this account

patch.description: Updates the four-digit PIN that future losing carriers must present to release this number. Requires that the number is currently on the authenticated account.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number owned by the authenticated account — used by number port out pin update.

requestBody.description: Four-digit PIN to set on this number for future port-out validation.

404.description: Number not on the authenticated account

error.message: Number not found or not on this account

post.description: Sends a text message. If mediaUrls is provided it goes as MMS via the messaging network; otherwise SMS via the SMS routing routed by the source number's assigned network. Charges the account on success.

requestBody.description: Source number, destination, and message text. Add mediaUrls to send MMS.

400.description: Required field missing or malformed (message send).

403.description: Source number is not on the authenticated account

error.message: Source number 2019085750 is not on this account

409.description: Messaging not enabled on this account, this number, or the recipient has opted out

502.description: Messaging gateway returned an unexpected response

get.description: Returns SMS or MMS Message Detail Records for a single number within a timestamp range. Both endpoints of the range are inclusive.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: sms (default), mms, or dlr. dlr returns delivery status events for sent messages.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number on the authenticated account — used by message history.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Unix timestamp range start (newer). Defaults to now.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Unix timestamp range end (older). Defaults to 24h ago.

error.message: Number is not on this account

get.summary: Read messaging state for a set of numbers

parameters.name: numbers
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of 10-digit numbers. Maximum 1000.

400.description: numbers query parameter missing or empty

parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number on the authenticated account — used by number messaging get.

error.message: At least one of routeIn or routeOut is required

403.description: routeIn does not belong to this account

error.message: routeIn 99999 is not a numbers_sms row on this account

404.description: Not Found response from number messaging patch.

error.message: Number not found or not on this account

post.description: Places an the number order for the listed numbers and adds them to the authenticated account. Charges accounts.cash by accounts.did × len(numbers). Supports the Idempotency-Key header for safe retries (the cached response is returned for 24h on replay).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Client-supplied opaque token. Retries with the same key return the cached response and do NOT re-charge or re-order.

requestBody.description: Numbers to order. Each may set an optional route (gateway_id on this account, default 4).

400.description: Validation failed before any carrier or DB write

error.message: orders accept at most 100 numbers per call

402.description: accounts.cash insufficient for the order total

error.message: Insufficient funds: order cost $5.0000 exceeds available $0.5000

502.description: the network rejected the order; no DB writes performed

get.summary: Check whether a number can be ported in
get.description: Checks whether the number can be ported in. On portable=true the system sets a Redis flag that lets a subsequent POST /v2.2/ports submit the port within an hour.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number to check.

error.message: number must be a 10-digit telephone number

502.description: the network did not return a usable response

post.summary: Register a 10DLC brand at the network
post.description: Brand registration. Body fields define the brand attributes.

error.message: Brand registration body must be a non-empty object

post.summary: Register a 10DLC campaign at the network
post.description: Campaign registration. messagingBrandId / externalCampaignId / campaignDescription are required; campaignClassName and campaignStartDate default to T / today if omitted.

400.description: Required field missing or network rejected the body

get.summary: Read campaign status from the messaging network
get.description: For each id, returns the the messaging network campaign status and the list of numbers currently bound.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated alphanumeric campaign ids; max 100.

put.summary: Assign a 10DLC campaign to a number
put.description: Binds the number to a registered campaign at the network. Number must be on the authenticated account.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number on the authenticated account — used by number messaging campaign assign.

requestBody.description: Campaign id to bind to this number.

404.description: Not Found response from number messaging campaign assign.

error.message: Number not found or not on this account

delete.summary: Unassign a number from a campaign
delete.description: Removes this number from the given campaign. The campaign continues to exist with its other bindings. campaignId is supplied as a query parameter (per REST: DELETE should not carry a request body).

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number on the authenticated account.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: TCR campaign id the number is being removed from.

error.message: Field Required: campaignId — the campaign the number is being removed from

404.description: Number not on this account

error.message: Number not found or not on this account

delete.summary: Unassign multiple numbers from a campaign
delete.description: Removes each listed number from the given campaign. The campaign itself is unchanged; only the number-to-campaign bindings are removed. campaignId is required — the system uses CSP sharing to resolve which messaging path holds the bindings, then issues path-appropriate removal calls.

error.message: Field Required: campaignId — the campaign each number is being removed from

409.description: Campaign is not on a supported messaging path

error.message: Campaign CXXXXXX is not on a supported messaging path: upstreamCnpId=Sxxxxxx (Unsupported upstreamCnpId)

parameters.name: numbers
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of 10-digit telephone numbers to remove from the campaign. Maximum 1000.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: TCR campaign id the numbers are being removed from. The network is resolved via CSP sharing.

tags.description: Account profile, settings, billing charges, credit history, call detail records, SIP registration status, and self-service signup and password recovery.
tags.name: Account

tags.description: Manage the IP Access Control List for IP-based SIP authentication. Only routable public IPv4 CIDRs with masks /8, /16, /24, or /32 are accepted.
tags.name: ACL

tags.description: Manage the account SIP authentication type (Digest, IP Auth, or both) and password.
tags.name: Authentication

tags.description: Provision and manage e911 emergency service records for telephone numbers on the account.
tags.name: e911

tags.description: Manage SIP gateways (outbound trunks). System route types (USER, T30, VOICE_BRIDGE, DID, T38, RTC_BRIDGE, SDN) are always present and read-only.
tags.name: Gateways

tags.description: Number ordering, search, porting, and messaging campaign provisioning.
tags.name: iNumbering

tags.description: Per-lookup billed services. CNAM returns the registered Caller ID Name. LRN returns Location Routing Number and carrier details. Requires the service to be enabled on the account.
tags.name: Lookups
Messaging
tags.description: SMS/MMS sending, routing, messaging campaigns, and delivery records.
Manage telephone numbers: routing, forwarding, DNIS translation, inbound CNAM lookup, outbound Caller ID Name (LIDB), SMS routing, and fax-to-email.
tags.name: Numbers

tags.description: Support ticket management. Customers see only their own tickets. Administrators see all tickets.
tags.name: Support

info.description: VoiceTel REST API. All resource identifiers are in the URL path. Authenticate every request with `Authorization: Bearer <apikey>`. To obtain your API key, POST `{"username": <id>, "password": "<password>"}` to /v2.2/account/apikey — this is the only endpoint that does not require an Authorization header. The response is `{"status":"success","data":{"apikey":"<32-hex-string>"}}`. Re-fetch the API key after any password change. Rate-limited endpoints: account/info, account/mrc, account/cdr, account/apikey — 6 requests per hour per IP.
info.title: VoiceTel API
